body.title: Domains are general and shared; a device owns its specialization — and specialization is graduating debt, folded back only when the general case PROVES the property
body.author: CC.0 (design intake with Akien)
body.text:
# D-domains-general-with-device-owned-specializations-2026-07-08
**title:** Domains are general and shared; a device owns its specialization — and specialization is graduating debt, folded back into the general case when the general case can PROVE the property
**date:** 2026-07-08
**status:** open
**author:** CC.0 (design intake with Akien)

## The shape

  unseen_university/domains/            GLOBAL domains — what a KIND of work means. Shared.
  devices/<device>/...                  the device's SPECIALIZATION of a domain. Owned by it.
  unseen_university/agentic/ (proposed) shared execution PRIMITIVES (loop, codecs, editor,
                                        block_apply, edit_format) — not a domain, not a proxy
  devices/inference/                    the PROXY: routing + dispatch, nothing else

A global CodingDomain says what coding IS. Each consumer that does coding inherits it and
specializes in its own way, and that specialization belongs to that consumer's device.

## Why the executor is the specialization axis

The agentic loop is NOT domain-universal — it is DS-specific. Aider brings its own build loop.
CC *is* a loop. Only DS needs the model driven turn-by-turn. Putting AgenticLoop on
BaseDomain.run() asserted 'every coding consumer must be driven', which is false for two of the
three consumers. The loop was misplaced twice: wrong layer (inside the inference proxy) and
wrong scope (on the general base).

The seam already exists: BaseDomain.run() is a template method — the escalation walk is the
invariant, `_run_attempt()` is the strategy. Its own docstring says a domain overrides
_run_attempt 'to change WHAT one attempt is ... WITHOUT touching the escalation walk'.

  CodingDomain (global)      leaves _run_attempt unimplemented
  DSCodingDomain             _run_attempt = AgenticLoop / ArchitectEditor   (devices/dicksimnel)
  AiderCodingDomain          _run_attempt = hand the ticket to aider        (devices/aider)
  CC                         instantiates no domain — CC is the executor

Note AgenticLoop cannot be DS-private: minion/tool_loop.py imports it too. It is a shared
execution primitive that DS's domain CONSUMES; it is not part of any domain.

## What is actually in the global base (the anti-ceremony test)

Today CodingDomain holds name='coding', critic_enabled=True, aci_mode=True, and the
architect/editor attempt. The last three are DS-specific — aider wants neither our critic nor
our ACI. Strip them and the 'global' base is nearly empty, which is the tell. If we cannot name
concrete things EVERY coding consumer shares, the base is ceremonial.

GLOBAL (proposed):  (a) the `domain` tag as a routing dimension; (b) the COMPLETION CONTRACT —
what counts as DONE for coding; (c) the core coding prompt.
DESCENDS TO DS:     critic_enabled, aci_mode, architect/editor split, harvest_mode, stuck_ladder.

## Specialization is graduating debt (Akien, 2026-07-08)

'We want domains to become LESS specialized over time — graduating to the general class. The
more powerful the reasoning engine, the more flexibility we have in what we give it. Every task
has its own balance. We create diversity only where we need it, and when we can fold the
diversity into the general case, we do.'

This is the gate-removal staircase applied to domain code: diversity is scaffolding; the proof
corpus earns its removal. It needs teeth, or it licenses premature generalization.

TODAY'S EVIDENCE (T-inference-migrate-consumers-cutover): the rule triples WERE a compensation.
They hand-curated devstral as the coding floor, guaranteeing 'coding runs on a tool-capable
model'. We folded that diversity into the general dimensional case because the general case
looked more principled. Nobody had written down the PROPERTY the compensation guaranteed, so
nobody proved the general case still held it. It did not: a tool-less reasoning model took the
coding slot and CONFABULATED completion — claimed it wrote a file it never wrote. That was an
UNEARNED FOLD-BACK, and it is the failure mode this decision exists to prevent.

### The mechanism: a Compensation record
Every specialization (an override, a flag, a crutch) records:
  - property        what it GUARANTEES        e.g. 'coding resolves to a tool-capable model'
  - incapacity      the executor weakness it assumes  e.g. 'local 24B cannot hold plan+edit in one pass'
  - graduation_proof  the runnable test that must pass ON THE GENERAL PATH before deletion

FOLD-BACK RULE: delete a compensation only when the general case PROVES the same property.
Not a judgment call — a red/green proof, exactly like proof-on-close.

Compensations must be ENUMERABLE (a registry), not merely overrides: a silent override never
announces that it is still there. Then we can ask 'DS coding carries N compensations; which are
graduatable?' and have a scraps job re-run their graduation proofs. Fold-back becomes a routine
instead of an act of heroism. Each fold-back is a question that got answered and became
structure (compiled inference).

### Direction of inheritance
The base is the GENERAL capability; a subclass may only ADD SCAFFOLDING, never add capability.
If a specialization contains something genuinely good (better completion checking, a smarter
stuck ladder), it is not a specialization — it is a PROMOTION CANDIDATE and belongs in the base.
Two motions: DESCEND (create diversity where an executor is weak) and PROMOTE/FOLD (when the
general case can carry it).

### Latent evidence this is already true in the tree
BaseDomain.aci_mode's own comment: 'the generalist base is a strong-tier passthrough -> off;
CodingDomain (weak local tier) -> on.' The code already knows the crutch exists because the tier
is weak — it simply never recorded the graduation condition. Same for critic_enabled and the
architect/editor split. And the fold-back APPARATUS is already built (eval_slice.py,
io_corpus.py, corpus_verdict.py, prereg.py, verdict_gate.py) — currently sitting in the routing
proxy, where it has no business being either; it belongs with the domain/eval layer.

## Consequences
- resolve_domain(name) (the global name->domain registry) and CapabilityMixin.capability_domain:str
  MUST GO: a name lookup cannot return DS's specialization AND aider's. device.py no longer calls
  resolve_domain (the cycle fix removed it), so capabilities/base.py is its ONLY remaining caller.
  An agent holds its own domain instance.
- devices/inference/domains/ is a WAYPOINT, not the destination (T-inference-move-agentic-loop-to-domain
  parked the loop there). Global domains move to unseen_university/domains/ (precedent:
  unseen_university/capabilities/ is already a top-level non-device package, so the single-import-root
  rule holds). Specializations move into their devices.
- FALSIFIABLE PREDICTION: as Hex's models improve, DS's specialization should SHRINK measurably. If it
  does not, either the models are not improving or a compensation is load-bearing for a reason we have
  not named.
- FALSIFIABLE PREDICTION: DS's device.py should visibly shrink, leaving lifecycle/shim, the bus
  listener, working memory, and tool interfaces. If it does not, the boundary is in the wrong place.

## Open questions for Akien
Q1: Nested control loops. If the global domain owns the escalation walk AND aider owns its own
    internal retry loop, there are two escalation authorities stacked (double-spend, double-halt —
    we just watched the walk misclassify a capability ceiling as an availability outage and retry it).
    CC.0 recommends: aider = a SINGLE attempt that our walk may escalate; aider does not own tier
    escalation. Confirm or overrule.
Q2: Home for the shared execution primitives (AgenticLoop, codecs, ArchitectEditor, block_apply,
    edit_format), used by DS AND minion. CC.0 proposes unseen_university/agentic/. Confirm.
Q3: Does the compensation registry ship now (with the domain split) or as a follow-on once there are
    at least two specializations to enumerate? CC.0 recommends: record the three fields in code from
    day one; the registry + scraps graduation job as a follow-on.

validity_conditions.note: CapabilityMixin resolves a GLOBAL domain by name (resolve_domain('coding')). Device-owned specializations make a name lookup unable to return DS's domain and aider's. The mixin's HAS-A-a-Domain split stands; its name-based resolution does not.

That decision made the Domain stand alone and own selection + prompts. Selection has since left the domain entirely (T-inference-break-proxy-domain-cycle: a routing method on a proxy-consumer created device->domains->agentic_loop->device). What remains domain-owned is execution + prompts, and execution is the SPECIALIZATION axis.

The unearned fold-back that motivates the compensation record: the rule triples guaranteed 'coding runs on a tool-capable model'; folding them into the general dimensional case without proving that property let a tool-less model confabulate completion.
